steps.title: Identify the structure at the blank
steps.content: The blank sits within an infinitive phrase: to [blank] their cars across France. The structure has the infinitive marker “to” directly followed by the verb, then the direct object their cars, then adverbial modifiers.

steps.title: Apply the infinitive rule and choose
steps.content:
An infinitive phrase requires the verb to attach directly to the infinitive marker and its object with no internal punctuation—the verb drive must connect immediately to their cars without any separator.

The other choices insert a period, colon, or semicolon that would break the infinitive construction.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
